Yes, make sure to use our custom agents, workflow and skills. Ask me if you have any questions. Also we have to make sure that the app is working as expected.

Hmmm. I thought that we changed the rules for discovery. My understanding was that if we ran a fresh discovery, then any and all jobs that have jobs.discovered_at older than 72 hours would get cascade removed. This was something that was establsihed to remove stale jobs. Are you telling me that this is not the case?
So, now, if I run a discovery, it will find jobs posted in the last 48 hours for all the sites? And it will also cascade remove all jobs that I have not yet applied for, but is in the database?
Give me a tldr of the summary document that was created for the workday fix
Ok, lets try to do this. Once this fix is made, can we sample query some workday jobs and see how many are being called?

Now another thing that I noticed with workday jobs was that there was no filter being applied to jobs from there. How can be beter grab jobs that are only relevant to us from workday? For example, I think one of the runs, about 3000 workday jobs were grabbed from accenture, for roles from all around the world.
yes, we should most definitely do that as well. Lets make sure all job extraction sites (from the discovery phase) is filtered correctly for age.
I mean '.copilot'

Yes, read the intructions-copilot.md in the manishslal/.github/ folder. There are other instructions in there as well. I need you (the main agent) to be able to spawn off multiple agents and do the work in a workflow so that there are minimal bugs.
what do you mean set up the pipeline integration?

Yes, implement this. And What happened to the comprehensive Option 3 implementation for scoring? Did we already complete that? If not, can we launch parallel agents to make both changes? When those changes are completed, make sure we run it through our pipeline and use the skills to move forward

While we are doing that, can you also look into the discovery process? Some jobs found from discovery are over 30 days old. Need to investigate this, as the requirements clearly have dictated that the jobs should be posted within 48 or 72 hours. Becuase of this filter not working correctly, there are thousands of jobs that are being rbought into the db. When we go to try and apply for them, their application link says expired or to that effect.

Lets try option 3. but during the implementation, it is important to keep in mind that all the other processes with ApplyPilot works without any issues. So make sure to use our agent pipeline. Read instructions if you need to. Leverage all the custom agents that are available and use the skills that have been added as well.

I am noticing that the scoring is too high for all jobs, need to be more realistic and grounded. Can you review the scoring that is being done and potentially see why the average score is high in general? What are some better ways to improve how we are scoring these jobs and to prevent each role getting a high score than should be? We want to find a fair and formidable middle ground.

So can we remove all the files that are related to this project that we do not currently need?
